methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Neuromeka, a South Korean robotics and manufacturing company, achieved public listing on KOSDAQ (ticker 277810.KQ) following $36.5M in total capital raised under founder Park Jong-hun's leadership. The valuation analysis operates within a constrained comp universe: one public peer and seven private robotics companies, including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

Peer-median valuation methodology proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables. The filtered peer set—requiring same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier matches—yielded fewer than three datapoints necessary for reliable triangulation. While seven robotics peers exist sector-wide, none satisfied the confluence of stage and tier constraints that would anchor a defensible multiple.

Given Neuromeka's public status and the absence of directly comparable trading data from peers at equivalent stages, traditional multiples-based approaches cannot be reliably applied. The $36.5M capital raised provides a historical funding floor but does not indicate current market valuation post-listing. A complete valuation assessment would require access to KOSDAQ pricing data, recent financials (revenue, EBITDA), and forward guidance—none of which is available in the current dataset. Until such inputs are sourced, quantitative valuation synthesis remains constrained.
